title: Peppery Mexican Sushi Rice Stir-fry
description: This dish combines the savory flavors of Norteno cuisine with crisp textures and a peppery kick to create a meal-prep friendly rice stir-fry, perfect for breakfast or lunch.

ingredients.name: Sushi Rice
ingredients.notes: Use a low-FODMAP variety if needed.

ingredients.name: Peanuts
ingredients.notes: Roasted and lightly crushed for texture

ingredients.name: Lime Juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed for a citrusy twist

instructions: Rinse the sushi rice under cold water and soak it in 2 cups of water for about 30 minutes.
Drain the soaked rice, rinse again briefly, then cook according to package instructions or microwave until tender. Let cool slightly.
In a large skillet, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add chopped cilantro and toasted peanuts; stir-fry for about 1 minute until heated through.
Add sliced green onions and red bell pepper into the skillet with the nuts and herbs. Stir-fry for another minute or so.
Pour in the chicken broth/vegetable stock, lime juice, and half a cup of cooked sushi rice into the skillet. Stir to combine all ingredients evenly.
Simmer gently over medium heat until most liquid is absorbed and the dish reaches desired consistency, about 5 minutes. Taste test for saltiness (optional).
Remove from heat; serve hot as a side dish or in wraps/rolls. Garnish with more cilantro if needed.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
